free_text: Total-reaction-cross section measurements for 30-60-MeV
protons and the imaginary optical potential.
All the topics were isotopically enriched to at least
90%. Their thicknesses ranged between 3 and 26 mg/cm2
with the exception of C-12 which was 46.7 mg/cm2 thick.
An accurate measurement of the target thickness
was made by measuring the energy loss with a magnetic
spectrometer
Three counters are Ne102 plastic scintillators
connected to amperex xp1110 photomultipliers.
A thin delta e detector subtending a small
solid angle with respect to the target and stopping
counter which detects protons scattered in the angular
range large 11. but less 45. degrees.
at 60.8 MeV.
at 60.8 MeV.
The relative error is 2% for the thick
targets and 5% for the thin targets.
No information about source of uncertainty
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents.
at 40 MeV.
Tbls II, III, IV, V from Phys.Rev.,C4(1971)1114
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
at 30 MeV.
at 49.5 MeV.
